reasoning: France are the clear market favorites at roughly +360, having won Group I with a perfect record and +8 goal difference behind an in-form Dembélé, and they boast the deepest squad able to absorb injuries. Argentina are the most dangerous challenger as reigning champions with a perfect group stage and a Messi on historic form (12 goals in his last 9 matches), sitting on the opposite side of the bracket for a plausible final meeting. Spain's title case is undermined by an injury crisis (Nico Williams, Yeremy Pino and Fermín López out plus Yamal fitness doubts), while Brazil look light up front without Estêvão and Rodrygo and over-reliant on a 34-year-old Neymar. The main threats to France are a potential last-16 clash with Germany and the inherent randomness of single-elimination knockouts decided by shootouts; Morocco, who pushed into the knockouts and face the Netherlands, are the live outsider given their World Cup pedigree and counter-attacking quality.
